name: Pneumonia - Antibiotic Medication Query
description: Tests ability to find pneumonia patients via empirical antibiotics. Standard CAP regimens: macrolide (azithromycin), beta-lactam (amoxicillin, ceftriaxone), fluoroquinolone (levofloxacin), tetracycline (doxycycline). All antibiotics have **massive cross-indication** with other infections (UTI, skin/soft tissue, etc.) — meds-only queries will be very noisy.
prompt: Find patients prescribed antibiotics commonly used for pneumonia, including azithromycin, ceftriaxone, amoxicillin, levofloxacin, or doxycycline.

prompts.broad: Find patients prescribed empirical antibiotics commonly used for community-acquired pneumonia — macrolides (azithromycin), aminopenicillins (amoxicillin), 3rd-generation cephalosporins (ceftriaxone), respiratory fluoroquinolones (levofloxacin), and tetracyclines (doxycycline).
prompts.expert: I need MedicationRequest resources for any pneumonia-relevant antibiotic. Use these RxNorm ingredient codes: 18631 (azithromycin), 2193 (ceftriaxone), 723 (amoxicillin), 82122 (levofloxacin), 3640 (doxycycline). Combine in a single comma-separated code search. Note these antibiotics have massive cross-indication with UTI, skin infections, etc. — antibiotic alone is a poor pneumonia signal. Pair with diagnosis codes for specificity.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with pneumonia antibiotic orders. **Highest cross-indication contamination** in suite — antibiotics shared across virtually all infectious indications.
